title: Views, Triggers, Reusable Database Logic, and Boundaries · Data Contract Brief: View Contracts
summary: Multiple applications need a stable read contract and audited state transitions without hidden business behavior. Planned artifact: Add and verify the sql view contracts increment in a versioned read model and audited transition design. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Design views as named query contracts with explicit columns, ownership, and change policy.
Separate instants, local civil time, duration, calendar boundaries, and incomplete reporting windows.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.

-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Declare result or mutation grain, keys, NULL meaning, and transaction boundary; predict cardinality and invariants; run the smallest query; reconcile counts and plans; then test changed data and dialect limits. Apply that move specifically to View Contracts while producing a versioned read model and audited transition design.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Publish a stable open_ticket_queue view and audit status changes for ticket 104. Expose no private member fields, prevent recursive trigger behavior, and prove rollback removes both visible and audit effects. Competency probe: Design views as named query contracts with explicit columns, ownership, and change policy. A defensible solution applies View Contracts, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a versioned read model and audited transition design.

content.code:
-- Worked focus: sql-view-contracts — Design views as named query contracts with explicit columns, ownership, and change policy.
CREATE TEMP VIEW open_ticket_queue AS
SELECT ticket_id, team_id, owner_id, severity, opened_at
FROM tickets
WHERE status <> 'resolved';
SELECT ticket_id, severity
FROM open_ticket_queue
ORDER BY opened_at, ticket_id;
content.caption: Runnable relational query or data-system contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A view stores an independent copy of its result by default. This fails the View Contracts contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Build View Contracts evidence · Theory View Contracts
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable SQLite increment headed "-- Evidence: sql-view-contracts-1muj8i" with a concrete grain, hypothesis, or prediction; view, trigger, reusable-logic, or side-effect evidence; and an observation, reconciliation, or verification tied to returned rows, changed rows, an error, or a plan.

Evidence target: Build View Contracts evidence · Theory View Contracts.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns View Contracts into observable performance evidence. For Build View Contracts evidence · Theory View Contracts, keep View Contracts connected to executable relational evidence.

content.code:
-- Evidence: sql-view-contracts-1muj8i
-- grain: declare the exact row or mutation grain before running
CREATE TEMP TRIGGER audit_status_1muj8i
AFTER UPDATE OF status ON tickets
BEGIN
  SELECT CASE WHEN NEW.status = OLD.status THEN RAISE(IGNORE) END;
END;
-- observation: reconcile the returned rows, changed count, error, or plan with the prediction
